organization.focus: Youth mental health and psychosocial well-being
organization.vision: To be a leading youth-focused organization shaping mentally healthy, resilient, and empowered generations while supporting vulnerable populations in Malawi and beyond.
organization.mission: To improve the mental health and psychosocial well-being of adolescents, youth, and vulnerable communities in Malawi and beyond through accessible services, community empowerment, research, and advocacy.

objectives.general: To promote holistic mental health, psychosocial well-being, and social resilience among vulnerable populations in Malawi and beyond through accessible services, community empowerment, research, and advocacy.
specific: Provide accessible, affordable, and quality mental-health counselling, therapy, and psychosocial support.
Raise awareness and promote mental-health education across community and institutional settings.
Prevent suicide and self-harm through prevention, early identification, referral, and peer-support structures.
Engage in advocacy and policy dialogue.
Strengthen community-based support systems through training.
Support children and youth affected by mental-health and socioeconomic challenges to remain in education.
Conduct and support research for evidence-based interventions and policy engagement.

programs.title: Empower Mind Chapters
institutions: Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences (MUBAS)
Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources (LUANAR)

programs.title: Mental Health Awareness Campaigns and Career Guidance in Schools

phases.title: University Engagement and Chapter Strengthening
activities: Strengthening coordination of existing Empower Mind chapters at UNIMA, MUBAS, Domasi, LUANAR, and CU
Engagement with university management and student representative bodies
Orientation and capacity-building meetings for chapter leaders and members

phases.title: School-Based Mental Health and Career Guidance
activities: Mental-health awareness campaigns in selected primary and secondary schools
Career-guidance and life-skills sessions for learners
Engagement with teachers and school administrations

phases.title: Suicide Prevention among University Students
activities: Roll-out of suicide-prevention interventions in universities
Awareness sessions, peer-support activities, and early identification of at-risk students
Participation in Suicide Prevention Month activities in September

phases.title: Official Launch of Empower Mind
activities: Planning and coordination of the official launch event
Invitation of government, academic institutions, civil society, partners, and youth stakeholders

phases.title: Stakeholder Engagement and Collaboration
activities: Continued engagement with stakeholders following the planned launch
Participation in relevant policy-dialogue platforms and technical meetings

accountability.reporting: Quarterly implementation reports to the Ministry of Health
